item.title: 'Phantom Squatting': An Emerging AI-Driven Supply Chain Threat
item.description:
LLMs consistently hallucinate Web domains for legitimate brands that attackers can register for malicious activity in a difficult-to-detect attack vector.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: When Too Much Security Data Becomes the Risk
item.description:
Rapid growth turned routine firewall logs into a security and budget liability. One CISO used artificial intelligence to filter what data truly belongs in the SIEM.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: And the Winner in Dominant Malware Delivery? ClickFix
item.description:
Researchers say the highly effective social engineering technique is no longer the exception for malware attacks — it's now the rule.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Crafty Phishing Campaigns Auto-Adapt to Victim's Device, OS
item.description:
Attackers fingerprint victims through user-agent data to deliver OS-specific payloads, increasing compromise rates and campaign profitability.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Ransomware Thugs Masquerade as Interpol to Entice Small Biz
item.description:
The ransomware campaign relies on basic social engineering and stretches across multiple regions, including the US, Europe, Middle East, and elsewhere.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: FortiBleed Actors Collaborating With Inc, Lynx Ransomware Gangs
item.description:
After gaining a foothold in thousands of Fortinet firewalls, the attackers are starting to monetize that access, and are also piling on a Nextcloud zero-day bug.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Aussies Face Reduced Cybercrime Risk, as Pressure Shifts to SMBs
item.description:
Improved institutional safeguards and stricter regulations have pushed the burdens of protection and risk reduction on to Australian businesses.
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Chinese LLMs Broaden the Gap Between Attackers & Defenders
item.description:
Two new models from Chinese firms compete with top US mainstream and frontier models. Should cyber-defenders be worried?
📰 Source: Dark Reading | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Trump Administration Lifts Restrictions on Anthropic’s Claude Models After Cybersecurity Alarm
item.description:
Anthropic said Tuesday night that its AI model called Claude Fable 5 is now widely available. The post Trump Administration Lifts Restrictions on Anthropic’s Claude Models After Cybersecurity Alarm appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: FortiBleed Campaign Linked to INC, Lynx Ransomware Attacks
item.description:
Researchers say credentials harvested from hundreds of thousands of FortiGate firewalls are being used to facilitate ransomware attacks by the INC and Lynx operations. The post FortiBleed Campaign Linked to INC, Lynx Ransomware Attacks appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: New CitrixBleed Vulnerability Exploited Immediately After Public Disclosure
item.description:
Hackers are targeting NetScaler appliances using public PoC code to retrieve arbitrary memory content in the HTTP response. The post New CitrixBleed Vulnerability Exploited Immediately After Public Disclosure appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Alleged Scattered Spider Hacker Extradited to US
item.description:
Prosecutors say 19-year-old Peter Stokes was a member of Scattered Spider, the hacking group linked to more than 100 network intrusions and over $100 million in ransom payments. The post Alleged Scattered Spider Hacker Extradited to US appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Medtronic Data Breach Impacts 3.8 Million People
item.description:
In April, ShinyHunters accessed the company’s corporate IT systems and stole patients’ personal and medical information. The post Medtronic Data Breach Impacts 3.8 Million People appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Agentic AI Used to Conduct Ransomware Attack via Langflow
item.description:
Attack demonstrates how LLM agents can combine known exploitation techniques with real-time reasoning to automate complex, multi-stage intrusions. The post Agentic AI Used to Conduct Ransomware Attack via Langflow appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: In Other News: Canadian Hacker Jailed, Open Source Zero-Days, Two Sentenced for ATM Jackpotting
item.description:
Noteworthy stories that might have slipped under the radar: Anonymous-linked Canadian hacker jailed, researcher drops zero-days in open source projects, Venezuelans sentenced in the US over ATM jackpotting. The post In Other News: Canadian Hacker Jailed, Open Source Zero-Days, Two Sentenced for ATM Jackpotting appeared first on SecurityWeek.
📰 Source: SecurityWeek | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: US lifts export controls on Anthropic’s frontier cybersecurity AI models
item.description:
Anthropic said export controls on certain models had been lifted after the company came to a series of agreements with the government.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Teen suspect in Scattered Spider hacks is extradited to US
item.description:
A complaint unsealed this week accuses a 19-year-old of participating in incidents including a breach of a "luxury-jewelry retailer" in 2025.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Supreme Court decision threatens EU-US data transfer agreement
item.description:
In a Tuesday letter, Max Schrems, the founder of the Vienna-based privacy advocacy organization noyb, told European officials he plans to sue to invalidate the EU-U.S. Data Privacy Framework (DPF) that allows for the transfer of personal data from the EU to U.S. companies.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Launch of UK's National Cyber Action Plan delayed amid Labour leadership crisis
item.description:
The plan had been due for publication on Monday, the sources said. It has been postponed amid the uncertainty over the governing Labour Party’s leadership contest, which opens July 9.
📰 Source: The Record | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: CISA Admin Leaked AWS GovCloud Keys on Github
item.description:
Until this past weekend, a contractor for the Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) maintained a public GitHub repository that exposed credentials to several highly privileged AWS GovCloud accounts and a large number of internal CISA systems. Security experts said the public archive included files detailing how CISA builds, tests and deploys software internally, and that it represents one of the most egregious government data leaks in recent history.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Lawmakers Demand Answers as CISA Tries to Contain Data Leak
item.description:
Lawmakers in both houses of Congress are demanding answers from the U.S. Cybersecurity & Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) after KrebsOnSecurity reported this week that a CISA contractor intentionally published AWS GovCloud keys and a vast trove of other agency secrets on a public GitHub account. The inquiry comes as CISA is still struggling to contain the breach and invalidate the leaked credentials.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Netherlands Seizes 800 Servers, Arrests 2 for Aiding Cyberattacks
item.description:
Authorities in the Netherlands have arrested the co-owners of two related Internet hosting companies for operating IT infrastructure used by Russia to carry out cyberattacks, influence operations and disinformation campaigns inside the European Union. The two men were the focus of a 2025 KrebsOnSecurity story about how their hosting companies had assumed control over the technical infrastructure of Stark Industries Solutions, an Internet service provider sanctioned last year by the EU as a frequ
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Hackers Used Meta’s AI Support Bot to Seize Instagram Accounts
item.description:
The Instagram accounts for the Obama White House and the Chief Master Sergeant of the U.S. Space Force were briefly defaced with pro-Iranian images and messages over the weekend, after instructions began circulating on Telegram showing how to trick Meta's "AI support assistant" bot into resetting account passwords.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Who Runs the Ransomware Group ‘The Gentlemen?’
item.description:
A cybercrime group known as The Gentlemen has emerged as the second most active ransomware gang by victim count, rapidly attracting a talented pool of hackers through an aggressive recruitment strategy that promises affiliates 90 percent of any ransom paid by victims. This post examines clues pointing to a real life identity for the administrator of The Gentlemen ransomware group.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Scattered Spider Hackers Plead Guilty on Day 1 of Trial
item.description:
Two men pleaded guilty in the United Kingdom this week to criminal charges stemming from an August 2024 cyberattack that crippled Transport for London, the entity responsible for the public transport network in the Greater London area. The duo were key members of a prolific cybercrime group known as Scattered Spider, and their guilty pleas came on the first day of what was expected to be a six-week trial.
📰 Source: Krebs on Security | 🏷️ Type: News

item.title: Verizon Releases Inaugural Breach Impact Study
item.description:
Verizon Business has released the findings from its inaugural Breach Impact Study, which focuses on the financial impact of data […] The post Verizon Releases Inaugural Breach Impact Study appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Serviceaide Pays $1.8 Million to Settle Data Breach Litigation
item.description:
Serviceaide, Inc., a provider of AI-powered solutions to boost productivity and enhance service delivery, has agreed to pay $1.8 million […] The post Serviceaide Pays $1.8 Million to Settle Data Breach Litigation appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Greater Rochester Independent Practice Association Settles MOVEit Data Breach Litigation
item.description:
A settlement has been agreed to resolve claims against Greater Rochester Independent Practice Association (GRIPA) arising from the May 2023 […] The post Greater Rochester Independent Practice Association Settles MOVEit Data Breach Litigation appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Employees Drop Class Action Lawsuit Against Stryker Over Hamdala Cyberattack
item.description:
A consolidated class action lawsuit against the medtech company Stryker over a March 2026 cyberattack has been voluntarily dismissed by […] The post Employees Drop Class Action Lawsuit Against Stryker Over Hamdala Cyberattack appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Delaware & Florida Women’s Health Centers Announce Data Breaches
item.description:
Two women’s healthcare providers have announced data privacy incidents. Women’s Wellness of Southern Delaware recently learned about unauthorized retention of […] The post Delaware & Florida Women’s Health Centers Announce Data Breaches appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: AdaptHealth Reports Material Cybersecurity Incident and Theft of Patient Data
item.description:
AdaptHealth, a publicly traded healthcare company that provides home medical equipment, diabetes supplies, and sleep therapy products, has informed the […] The post AdaptHealth Reports Material Cybersecurity Incident and Theft of Patient Data appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Almost 30,000 Texas Residents Affected by Data Breach at The Texas Hearing Institute
item.description:
The Texas Hearing Institute has notified the Texas Attorney General about a data breach impacting more than 29, 000 state […] The post Almost 30,000 Texas Residents Affected by Data Breach at The Texas Hearing Institute appeared first on The HIPAA Journal.
📰 Source: HIPAA Journal | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: Unknown
item.description:
Unknown reported a healthcare breach.
📰 Source: HHS OCR | 🏷️ Type: Healthcare Breach

item.title: FortiBleed credential-theft campaign linked to Lynx ransomware
item.description:
The massive FortiBleed credential theft campaign has been linked to the INC and Lynx ransomware operations, suggesting the stolen Fortinet credentials were intended to fuel future network intrusions. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Medtronic notifies customers impacted by ShinyHunters data breach
item.description:
Healthcare device firm Medtronic is notifying affected customers about a data breach that exposed their personal data to an unauthorized third party. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Alleged Scattered Spider hacker extradited to the United States
item.description:
A dual United States and Estonian citizen has been extradited to the U.S. to face charges alleging he was a member of the Scattered Spider hacking collective. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Opera rolls out Paste Protect feature to fight ClickFix attacks
item.description:
Opera has introduced Paste Protect, a security feature designed to block ClickFix-style attacks that trick users into executing malicious commands through social engineering. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: Cisco finally confirms attackers exploiting Unified CM flaw
item.description:
Cisco confirmed that attackers are now exploiting a Unified Communications Manager (Unified CM) vulnerability patched in early June. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: JadePuffer ransomware used AI agent to automate entire attack
item.description:
Researchers identified what they believe is the first documented case of a ransomware operation, JadePuffer, conducted entirely by a large language model (LLM) agent. [...]
📰 Source: BleepingComputer | 🏷️ Type: Security News

item.title: EXCLUSIVE: Top-100 Law Firm Fox Rothschild Suffers Data Breach and Leak by Silent Ransom Group
item.description:
Fox Rothschild is a top-100 law firm whose articles and resources have been cited on DataBreaches.net and PogoWasRight.org dozens of times over the years. This time, however, they are the subject of a post because they were victims of a data breach by a well-known group that targets law firms. Introduction The group called Silent... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Insurance giant Aflac discloses data breach at Japan subsidiary
item.description:
Sergiu Gatlan reports: American insurance giant Aflac has disclosed a new data breach after attackers breached its Japan subsidiary’s systems and stole personal and bank account information. Aflac (short for American Family Life Assurance Company) is a Fortune 500 company and the largest supplemental insurance provider in the United States, serving millions of customers in... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: The Fall of XSS Forum: From DaMaGeLaB to the 2025 takedown
item.description:
Ransomnews has published a history and analysis of XSS Forum from its inception to its seizure in 2025. There is so much that is interesting and informative in their report that it’s hard to know what to mention here, but here are just two portions below: As an overview: XSS.is, the most influential Russian-language cybercrime... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: The Human Element: Building A Trusted Workforce in the Age of DPRK Employment Fraud
item.description:
From Nisos: Earlier this year, our DPRK employment fraud investigation revealed how North Korean operatives infiltrate US companies at industrial scale. In June, we released Part 2 of our research, featured on Nicole Perlroth’s “To Catch a Thief” podcast, that takes you inside the actual operations of a DPRK cell. When a suspicious candidate applied to... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Kaspersky Lab experts have discovered a new attack vector and toolkit for compromising corporate Gmail accounts
item.description:
Kaspersky Labs writes: It is used by the ToddyCat group. Kaspersky Lab experts have discovered a new attack vector and toolkit for compromising corporate Gmail accounts. Using this toolkit, attackers can access user accounts via an API, read conversations, and harvest data from calendars and other Google services while remaining undetected for extended periods of... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: UK journalists and NGOs risk terrorism prosecutions under new security bill
item.description:
MEE reports: New national security legislation being rushed through the UK’s parliament could criminalise British foreign correspondents and NGO workers engaging with designated state-backed groups, experts warn. The National Security (State Threats) Bill, which is moving through its final stages in parliament this week, hands the UK Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood sweeping powers to designate as a threat any... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: Global Schools Holdings Cites Two Injunctions in a Bid to Chill Our Reporting. It Won’t Work.
item.description:
My About page is pretty clear about legal threats: If you want to send me legal threats about my reporting or comments, knock yourself out, but don’t be surprised to see me report on your threat, any confidentiality sig blocks you may attach notwithstanding. I have been threatened with lawsuits many times, and to be... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: HK: Shun Hing Group data breach affects 920,000 customers, 1.05m files encrypted in cyber attack
item.description:
Erwin Wong reports: Shun Hing Group has confirmed that its computer systems were compromised by hackers in March, resulting in a significant data breach affecting customers and staff. Founded in 1953 by the late Dr William Mong, Shun Hing Group has grown into a leading and diversified Hong Kong conglomerate, best known as the sole... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: An AI just carried out a cyber attack without any human oversight for the first time
item.description:
Anthony Cuthbertson reports: Security researchers have uncovered what they believe to be the first ever instance of an artificial intelligence agent executing a cyber attack from start to finish without human assistance. The AI-powered attack marks a major milestone for both artificial intelligence and cyber security, raising concerns that AI is lowering the barrier for cyber criminals. The fully automated campaign involved... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: AdaptHealth says attackers sweet-talked their way into cloud systems and stole patient data
item.description:
Connor Jones reports: AdaptHealth says attackers used social engineering to breach its systems and steal sensitive patient data, including passwords associated with insurance billing. The medical equipment company disclosed the attack to the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) on Thursday, noting that attackers accessed internal patient management systems, document storage platforms, and external electronic health record system... Source
📰 Source: DataBreaches.net | 🏷️ Type: Data Breach

item.title: AssetMark, Inc.
